title: <div>  •  📅 Past Event (Before the text’s publication): A historical event that
content:
• 📅 Past Event (Before the text’s publication): A historical event that influenced its themes
• 📅 Present Event (At the time of the text’s publication): A contemporary issue it responded to
• 📅 Future Projection: How this theme continues to evolve in today’s world

Example:
📖 Week 3 – Political Storytelling & The Novel’s Rise
• 📅 Past: Ancient political propaganda in early civilizations
• 📅 Present (At the Novel’s Birth): The role of newspapers & novels in revolutions
• 📅 Future: How misinformation & AI-generated narratives manipulate today’s world

✅ Why This Works:
• Shows literature as a living, evolving force.
• Encourages students to see storytelling as an active participant in history.

🎭 5. Catharsis Threads

Every week, students engage in a structured “Day” (Concrete) or “Night” (Abstract) reflection.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 14 (chunk 14/555)
